title: lupinus benthamii
intro: Lupinus benthamii, also known as the Bentham's lupin, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. Native to the southwestern region of Western Australia, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Lupinus benthamii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Lupinus benthamii can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including skin issues and gastrointestinal problems.
